---
name: Propose a Signal
about: Document a real developer pain point with community evidence. Approved signals become claimable ideas.
title: "[SIGNAL] "
labels: signal, needs-review
assignees: SidharthKriplani
---

## The pain in one sentence
<!-- What were you trying to do, and what broke or was missing? -->

## Community evidence
<!-- At least one link required. Reddit thread, HN post, GitHub issue, Stack Overflow — with upvote/comment counts. -->
<!-- Example: https://reddit.com/r/LocalLLaMA/... — 87 upvotes, 41 comments -->

## What you tried
<!-- Existing tools, workarounds, manual steps. Be specific about why each fell short. -->

## Gap verification
<!-- Spend 10 minutes searching. Does a fully-solving, zero-friction tool already exist for this exact problem? -->
<!-- If yes: name it and close this issue. That's also a contribution. -->
<!-- If no: explain what the closest existing tools are and where they fall short. -->

## What a solution would look like
<!-- A focused description of the tool that would solve this. CLI? Web app? Library? Zero-dependency? -->

---
<!-- What happens next:
     - Maintainer reviews evidence and gap verification
     - If real + gap verified → labeled `signal-approved`
     - You (or anyone) can then add it to SIGNALS.md + IDEAS.md
     - After that, claim it with: python3 track.py --claim SIGNAL-XXX --github your-username
     - Signals without community evidence are not approved. -->
